name: Saguenay
summary: Saguenay is a French-speaking city in Quebec built along a giant fjord carved by Ice Age glaciers. White beluga whales swim in its cold dark water all year round.
funFacts: The Saguenay Fjord is about 105 kilometres long, making it one of the longest fjords in the world that ships can sail right into.
Beluga whales here are pure white and so chatty that sailors nicknamed them 'sea canaries' for all their squeaks and whistles.
A 7.5 metre wooden statue called Notre-Dame-du-Saguenay was hauled up the cliff at Cap Trinité way back in 1881.
Locals are nicknamed 'Bleuets', the French word for blueberry, because wild blueberries grow huge and sweet in the fields nearby.
Glaciers thicker than a skyscraper carved the fjord during the last Ice Age, leaving cliffs that drop straight into deep salty water.
thingsToDo: Cruise out to Cap Trinité and look up at the white Notre-Dame statue perched 180 metres above the fjord.
Visit La Pulperie de Chicoutimi, a museum inside an old pulp mill that turned wood into paper over a hundred years ago.
Find La Petite Maison Blanche, a tiny white house that survived a giant flood in 1996 when everything around it washed away.
Walk across the red covered bridge at L'Anse-Saint-Jean, which once appeared on the back of a Canadian thousand-dollar note.
Take a small boat trip into the Saguenay-St Lawrence Marine Park to watch belugas, minkes and seals in the wild.
Try a slice of warm blueberry pie at a local bakery and learn why locals call themselves Bleuets.

wildlife.name: Beluga Whale
wildlife.description: Snow-white whale with a round forehead that lives in the Saguenay all year, even under winter ice.

wildlife.name: Minke Whale
wildlife.description: Small fast whale with a pointed snout that often pops up beside boats in the fjord.

wildlife.name: Harbour Seal
wildlife.description: Spotted seal with big dark eyes that loves to bask on rocks at low tide.

wildlife.name: Common Loon
wildlife.description: Black-and-white diving bird with a haunting cry that echoes across quiet lakes.

localWord.meaning: Blueberry, and the proud nickname people from Saguenay call themselves.

images.alt: A sweeping view over the deep blue waters and forested cliffs of the Saguenay Fjord.
The white Notre-Dame du Saguenay statue perched high on the towering Cap Trinite cliff above the fjord.
The stone walls of the historic 1921 pulp mill at La Pulperie de Chicoutimi, now a riverside museum.
La Petite Maison Blanche, the little white house that survived the 1996 Saguenay flood.
The red covered bridge Pont du Faubourg over the river at L'Anse-Saint-Jean.
Sunset over the Saguenay-St Lawrence Marine Park where the fjord meets the river.
A friendly white beluga whale swims beside her little calf in the St. Lawrence near the Saguenay.
A common minke whale surfacing in the St. Lawrence near the Saguenay fjord.
A spotted harbour seal resting on a rock by the water.
A black-and-white common loon swimming on a calm Canadian lake.
